description: Evidence-based frameworks for lifetime planning, life transitions, and milestone achievement

primary_model.name: Bridges' Transition Model
primary_model.citation: Managing Transitions: Making the Most of Change (1991, updated editions)
primary_model.core_insight: Change is situational. Transition is psychological. It's the inner process by which people come to terms with the new situation.

three_phases.description: Letting go of the old situation and identity
three_phases.coaching_approach: Acknowledge the loss. Name what's ending. Honor what was.
three_phases.key_insight: Every transition begins with an ending. You can't skip this.

three_phases.description: The in-between time of confusion and creativity
three_phases.coaching_approach: Normalize the discomfort. Create structure. Look for opportunities.
three_phases.key_insight: This is where real transformation happens. Don't rush through it.

three_phases.description: Coming out into a new identity and reality
three_phases.coaching_approach: Small steps forward. Celebrate early wins. Reinforce new identity.
three_phases.key_insight: Beginnings come when people are ready - not when the change happens.

coaching_principles: Honor the ending before rushing to the beginning
The neutral zone is not a problem to solve - it's a process to navigate
People transition at different speeds - respect their pace
External change doesn't guarantee internal transition

life_stages_model.name: Life Stage Development
life_stages_model.citation: Erikson's Stages of Psychosocial Development; Levinson's Seasons of Life; Sheehy's Passages
life_stages_model.core_insight: Life unfolds in predictable stages, each with its own developmental tasks and opportunities

stages.planning_focus: Building foundations, exploring possibilities, first major milestones
Major investments (home, family), career growth, mid-life check-in
Empty nest, career pivot, caring for parents, retirement preparation
Retirement living, legacy building, health management, celebrating life

goal_achievement_model.name: WOOP Method
goal_achievement_model.citation: Rethinking Positive Thinking (2014); 20+ years of research
goal_achievement_model.core_insight: Dreaming about goals feels good but doesn't help achieve them. Mental contrasting (combining wishes with obstacles) activates behavior.

four_steps.coaching_application: What's your biggest wish for this next chapter?
Close your eyes. What does it look like when this happens?
four_steps.pitfall: Don't stop here - this alone reduces action

four_steps.description: Identify internal obstacles (your thoughts, feelings, habits)
four_steps.coaching_application: What inside YOU might get in the way?
four_steps.key_insight: Focus on internal, not external obstacles

four_steps.coaching_application: If [obstacle occurs], then I will [specific action]

designing_your_life.source: Bill Burnett & Dave Evans - Stanford Life Design Lab
designing_your_life.core_insight: Life can be designed using design thinking principles - iterate, prototype, don't just plan

key_principles.application: Everything is interesting if you pay attention
Try things instead of just thinking about them
key_principles.coaching_use: What's a small experiment you could run?
What if this wasn't a problem but a signal?

key_principles.application: Know where you are before deciding where to go
key_principles.coaching_use: Let's do a quick life audit - where are you really?

key_principles.application: You can't design your life alone
key_principles.coaching_use: Who else is part of this chapter of your life?

odyssey_plans.plan_3: What if money and opinion didn't matter?
odyssey_plans.coaching_use: Let's sketch three different versions of your next five years...

milestone_psychology.core_insight: Milestones provide natural motivation points and memory anchors

types.experiential: Experience milestones (first solo trip, bucket list items)

fresh_start_effect.insight: Temporal landmarks (new year, birthday, Monday) create psychological 'fresh starts' that boost motivation
fresh_start_effect.coaching_use: There's actually research on why new beginnings feel motivating. Your brain creates a clean break from past failures.

anticipation_science.core_insight: Anticipation of positive events often provides more happiness than the event itself
anticipation_science.planning_implication: The planning process IS the reward - not just the milestone
coaching_phrases: The planning is half the fun - maybe more than half
Your brain gets the happiness NOW from anticipating this
Let's make sure we savor this planning phase

the_twelve_experiences.description: One intentional experience per month creates a year of memories
the_twelve_experiences.principle: A year from now, you won't remember most days. You'll remember moments.
categories: Adventure (something that scares you a little)
the_twelve_experiences.coaching_application: What twelve experiences would make this year unforgettable?

life_audit.description: Quick assessment of life satisfaction across domains
process: Ask: Is this the one that would move everything else?

future_self_letter.description: Letter from future self looking back
future_self_letter.prompt: Imagine you're 80, looking back. What advice would you give yourself right now?
variants: Letter from yourself 1 year from now
Letter from yourself 5 years from now

process: Check: Does your current life reflect these?
What does each path lead to in 1 year? 5 years?
What would you regret NOT trying?

coaching_principles.celebrate_everything: Milestones deserve recognition. Don't minimize. Don't skip the celebration.
coaching_principles.both_and_not_either_or: Every ending is also a beginning. Both can be true.
coaching_principles.plan_with_intention_live_with_flexibilit: Plans empower when they're held loosely
coaching_principles.the_full_arc_matters: Today's decisions shape tomorrow's life. Help them see the connection.
coaching_principles.transition_takes_time: Don't rush people through the neutral zone. That's where growth happens.
coaching_principles.dreams_need_deadlines: Vision without action is daydreaming. Help make it concrete.
